title: Babka
ingredients: 530g all-purpose flour, plus extra for dusting
150g unsalted butter (room temp, cut into 2cm cubes)

title: Challah
ingredients: 78g oil (either olive oil or a neutral oil)
Poppy seeds (can swap this out for sesame seeds, or use a mix)

title: Shio pan
ingredients: 90g salted butter (split into 6 pieces, each 2cm x 4cm)

title: Sourdough
ingredients: 166g hard red wheat flour (optional, use 766g bread flour otherwise)

title: Chocolate mirror glaze
ingredients: 130g cocoa powder (regular or dutch processed for richer flavour)

title: Flourless chocolate cake
ingredients: 170g bittersweet chocolate (50% cocoa solids or higher, chopped)
170g bittersweet chocolate (50% cocoa solids or higher, chopped)

title: Rainbow mirror cake
ingredients: 4-5 coloured [[sponge cake]] layers (9 inches or 23 cm in diameter)

title: Cantonese-style bao
ingredients: 1/2 tsp ammonia bicarbonate (if not available, use 3/4 tsp baking powder above instead)
1 egg lightly beaten or 6g vegetable oil

title: Hong Kong style roast duck
ingredients:
15g five spice powder 
4.5g szechuan peppercorns 
4g cinnamon stick 
4g fennel seed 
2g star anise 
0.5g cloves
1 spring onion (green parts, chopped into 3 inch pieces)

title: Red braised [protein]
ingredients: 1 spring onion (white part cut into 2 inch sections)

title: Sichuan lamb noodle soup
ingredients: 1.5 to 1.8kg (approximately 4) lamb shanks or lamb neck slices
2 tbsp cumin seeds (toasted and coarsely ground)

title: Blue moon ice cream
ingredients: 1/2 tsp orange blossom water (or other aromatics, such as vanilla essence, almond extract, rose water, or orange blossom water)1.

title: Burnt orange ice cream
ingredients: 1 vanilla bean (split lengthwise — or substitute with 1 tsp vanilla paste)

title: Chocolate sorbet
ingredients: 170g bittersweet or semisweet chocolate, finely chopped

title: Palak paneer
ingredients: 2 tsp cumin seeds (toasted and roughly pounded in a mortar and pestle)

title: Kaketsuyu (hot noodle soup)
ingredients: 1L [[awase dashi]] (seasoned with 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tbsp mirin) or 250ml [[mentsuyu (noodle soup base) | mentsuyu]] (diluted with 750ml water).
keywords: Japanesechicken beef soba udon dashi prawn

title: Soy sauce eggs
ingredients: 4 - 5 large eggs (plus more in case any of the eggs crack!)
1/4 cup (60g) dark soy sauce (can be substituted with an equal amount of regular soy sauce)

title: Corned silverside beef with cabbage, white sauce, and onions
ingredients: 1 large head of cabbage (optional, cut into quarters)

title: Latkes
ingredients: 1 medium yellow onion, peeled and quartered
Applesauce and sour cream, for serving

title: Crab and scallop spaghetti in a white wine sauce
Lobster and miso tortellini
ingredients: 240g lobster meat (approx. from 1 large or 3 small tails; reserve shells for bisque)
Small fresh basil leaves, to garnish

title: Prawn and tomato spaghetti with bisque
ingredients: Salt, pepper, and MSG, to taste

title: Turkey sausage
ingredients: 500g turkey mince (can also use beef mince)

title: Apple tarte tatin with crème diplomat
ingredients: 165g unsalted butter (shaped into a 10 cm square, slightly softened)
3 medium granny smith apples (peeled and halved, core removed)
Oven temperatures are for conventional; if using fan-forced (convection), reduce the temperature
by 20˚C. We use Australian tablespoons and cups: 1 teaspoon equals 5 ml; 1 tablespoon equals 20
ml; 1 cup equals 250 ml. All herbs are fresh (unless specified) and cups are lightly packed. All
vegetables are medium size and peeled, unless specified. All eggs are 55-60 g, unless specified.

title: Blueberry pie

keywords: Pastrypate sablee dough tart crust shortcrust

Braised lamb shoulder chops with rice
Roast leg of lamb

title: Roast potatoes
ingredients: 75ml extra-virgin olive oil (or duck, chicken, or beef fat)
Small handful picked rosemary leaves, finely chopped

title: Beef rendang
ingredients: 0.8-1kg boneless beef short ribs (cut into 2 inch cubes)1
1 cup thick coconut milk (Ayam brand preferred)
2 teaspoons tamarind pulp soaked in some warm water and strained (alternatively jarred tamarind pulp is fine).
1 tablespoon sugar/palm sugar or to taste
10-12 dried chilies (soaked in warm water and seeded)

title: Fish head curry
ingredients: 1 tbsp sugar (ideally palm sugar, gula melaka)
4 whole dried chilies or 1 tsp chilli powder

title: Kailan in oyster sauce
ingredients: 1 tsp minced garlic (or chicken rice ginger)

title: Kerisik
ingredients: Whole coconut or grated desiccated coconut (if using grated coconut, go straight to step 3)

title: Quick kaya
ingredients: 2 pandan leaves (knotted) or 1/2 tsp pandan essence

title: Roti prata
ingredients: 500g bread flour (aim for flour with 11% protein)
60ml milk, plus extra if necessary (1/4 cup)
1 tbsp caster sugar, or to taste

title: Bamya assam
ingredients: 1.2kg of chicken legs/thighs (about 8 thighs or 4 marylands)

title: Beetroot stew
ingredients: 1.2kg of chicken legs/thighs (about 8 thighs or 4 marylands)

ingredients: 500g minced chicken (could also use beef or lamb)

title: Yellow rice
ingredients: Chicken bones (approximately 1 backbone, a few wings, etc.)

title: Awase dashi
ingredients: 30g niboshi (dried sardines, heads and guts removed)

title: Chicken stock
ingredients: 2 large (85g) celery rib (cut into 2 inch pieces)

title: Curry leaf mayonnaise
ingredients: 14 cardamom pods (pods discarded and seeds crushed or ground).
20 fresh curry leaves (finely chopped or ground)
120 ml sunflower oil (or, optionally, replace with debittered mustard oil)
2 tbsp (30 ml) lime juice or rice vinegar

title: Matzah ball soup
ingredients: 1/2 cup melted schmaltz or vegetable oil
1 tbsp chopped dill, plus more for serving

title: Miso soup
ingredients: 1L of [[awase dashi]] (ideally niban dashi, or second dashi)1

title: Pesto
ingredients: 200g spinach (or other combinations of greens, such as basil, rocket, kale, etc.)
